text: Starting a marathon too fast leads to endurance problems later in the race and poorer overall finish times.
Finishing a marathon too fast suggests overly cautious pacing earlier in the race, costing overall finish time.
Women tend to pace their marathon more effectively than men, maintaining more even splits throughout the race.
Women can be overly cautious in their marathon pacing, costing them minutes on their overall finish time.
Even pacing (avoiding both too-fast starts and too-cautious early pacing) optimizes marathon finish times for recreational runners.
Fast starts in marathons tend to be very fast rather than moderately fast, amplifying the negative impact on later race performance.
